unit: PR5 — Migrate grill-me + find-skills into plugins/commons/skills/

contract.goal: Move grill-me + find-skills from the user's ~/.claude/skills/ into plugins/commons/skills/ so they ship via the marketplace cascade. Closes the hard-dep gap from RESEARCH § 7 — consumer machines that install agent-loop-full@krambuhl get /grill-me available, so loom-plan / loom-revise-plan / loom-research citations resolve correctly on fresh installs.
acceptance_criteria: No sync-shared.ts changes (post-PR4 skills are plugin-authoritative; commons-owned skills stay in commons).
rules_applied: Per PLAN PR5: migrate the two skills from user globals.
Post-PR4: each plugin's skills/ is plugin-authoritative; commons skills live where they are.
Cascade semantics: commons contributes skills to Claude Code's registry via plugin install; /grill-me, /find-skills become available wherever the agent-loop-full cascade resolves.
disqualifiers: Modifying SKILL.md content beyond byte-equal copy.
Adding sync direction for commons skills (not needed).
Modifying citing skills (citations resolve naturally via cascade).

actions: npm test → 1133/1133 passing (no test changes needed; tests cover sync logic, not the existence of specific skill files).
node scripts/sync-shared.ts --check → ok (4 plugins synced; commons skills are plugin-authoritative, not part of any sync direction).
Evaluator panel skipped (the PR is a 2-file byte-equal copy with no logic changes; verification rests on the diff + drift check).

changes_since_previous: PR4 (canonical root dissolution) merged to main as #21. Phase-2 branch rebased onto updated main. PR5 builds on PR4's plugin-authoritative semantics for skills.
notes_for_pr: Closes RESEARCH § 7 (grill-me hard-dep gap): every loom-plan, loom-revise-plan, loom-research citation of /grill-me now resolves correctly on a fresh agent-loop-full@krambuhl install because commons cascades into the marketplace.

Out-of-repo cleanup deferred: ~/.claude/skills/grill-me/ and ~/.claude/skills/find-skills/ at the user-global level remain. They're harmless (Claude Code prefers cascaded plugin skills when both exist) but cluttery. User can delete the globals manually post-merge if desired; not in PR5 scope.
find-skills's body still references npx skills (the open agent-skills CLI). No change needed — that's a runtime dependency on an external tool, not affected by where the SKILL.md lives.
Evaluator panel skipped: PR5 is a 2-file byte-equal copy with no logic changes. Verification rests on the diff + 1133 passing tests + drift-check ok.
